response.justification: The candidate brings nearly two decades of broad engineering experience, current Staff-level work on AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling), and genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (microservices handling millions of requests per second). However, the must-have of strong Python is not evidenced anywhere on the resume — her performance work is in DLang and her product work in TypeScript/Node — and evidence of directing technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder management at scale is thin, with most roles reading as hands-on senior IC positions.
strengths: Current hands-on work in AI-native engineering: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Strong craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tools authorship, and teaching/workshops
concerns: No Python listed or demonstrated, which is a must-have for the role
Limited evidence of technical-strategy ownership and stakeholder coordination at staff scope
Significant investment in niche DLang ecosystem; recent roles skew toward product full-stack rather than large-scale reliability engineering
